info.title: REST API for Common Features in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications - Resource Types
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.

Some REST resources have more than one version. In the Tasks section, when you expand a resource that has more than one version, you'll notice that the main folder contains subfolders, such as 11.13.18.05 and v1 (latest), corresponding to the available resource versions. Resources that don't have a newer version continue to appear without any change in their folder structure. You can find additional details on how to work with the different resource versions in the Quick Start and other topics in the Get Started section.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the resource type.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

id.title: Id
id.description: The identifier of the resource type.

meta.title: Meta
meta.description: The metadata of a resource type.

schemas.title: Schemas
schemas.description: A list of schema values that contain supported schema version for a SCIM representation, and any schema extensions for that representation. Each value must be a unique URI.

schemaExtensions.title: SchemaExtensions
schemaExtensions.description: A list of URIs of the schema extensions for a resource type. A schema extension contains any extended schema attributes, which are identified by the schema extension URI.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the resource type. This attribute is referenced by the meta.resourceType attribute in all the resources.

description.title: Description
description.description: The description of the resource type.

endpoint.title: Endpoint
endpoint.description: The endpoint of the resource type. For example, /Users.

schema.title: Schema
schema.description: The primary schema of the resource type. This must be equal to the id attribute of the associated schema resource.
